content: Synthesis: How to synthesize CsPbBr₃ nanocubes at room temperature?

This task involves developing a synthesis strategy for CsPbBr₃ nanocubes synthesized at room temperature. It involves designing a suitable synthesis method by consulting with the "Synthesis Scientist" in the Scientist team. Once the synthesis plan is ready, it needs to be visualized and converted into JSON format for execution by the Executor. I will plan and delegate tasks accordingly.

content.content:
[{'id': None, 'title': 'sun-et-al-2016-ligand-mediated-synthesis-of-shape-controlled-cesium-lead-halide-perovskite-nanocrystals-via.md', 'content': '

Synthesis of CsPbBr3 Nanocubes
N,N Dimethylformamide (2 mL) and PbBr2 (0.1 mmol) were loaded into a 5 mL flask. Oleic acid (1.55 mmol) and dodecylamine (0.086 mmol) were added. After complete solubilization of PbBr2, Cs−oleate solution (0.1 mL, 0.4 M in ODE, prepared as described above) was added. Then, 0.05 mL of hybrid solution was added into a 5 mL flask along with toluene (2 mL).', 'metadata': None, 'embedding': None, 'score': None}, {'id': None, 'title': 'Flexible All-Inorganic Perovskite Photodetector with a Combined Soft-Hard Layer Produced.md', 'content': 'Supporting Information
**Flexible All-Inorganic Perovskite Photodetector with a Combined Soft-Hard Layer Produced by Ligand Cross-Linking**

Tongyu Shi1,2, Xi Chen1,2, Rui He1\*, Hao Huang1, Xinru Yuan1,2, Zhenyu Zhang1, Jiahong Wang $I,2,3$ , Paul K. Chu4, Xue-Feng Yu1,2,3\* 

**METHOD**

Materials. The main chemical reagents including cesium bromide (CsBr, Aladin, $99.9\%$ ), lead bromide (PbBr2, Aladin, $99.0\%$ ), N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF, Aladin, $99.9\%$ ), oleylamine (OAm, Aladin, $\mathrm{C}18{:}80{-}90\%$ ), oleic acid (OA, Aladdin, AR), 1H,1H,2H,2H-Perfluorodecyltrichlorosilane (FDTS, Aladin, $96\%$ ), chloroform (Sinopharm, $99.0\%$ ), ethonal (Sinopharm, $99.8\%$ ), acetone (Sinopharm, $99.0\%$ ), gold (Zhongnuo Advanced Material (Beijing) Technology Co., Ltd, $99.99\%$ ) were used without purification, except chloroform which was dehydrated before use. 

Synthesis of purified CsPbBr3 NCs. The CsPbBr3 NCs were prepared by the ligandassisted reprecipitation method. Firstly, CsBr (0.4 mmol), $\mathrm{PbBr}_{2}$ ( $\leftmoon.4\mathrm{\mmol}\right)$ ) were dissolved in DMF $\mathrm{[0\,mL]}$ ). Subsequently, OAm $\left(0.1\,\mathrm{mL}\right)$ ) and OA (1 mL) were added to stabilize the precursor solution until the powder was completely dissolved. 1 mL of the precursor solution was added quickly into chloroform ( $\mathrm{[0\mL]}$ under vigorous stirring, and strong green emission was observed immediately. After three times centrifugation ( $10{,}000\;\mathrm{rpm}$ , $10\;\mathrm{min}$ ), purified CsPbBr3 NCs can be obtained. 

Synthesis of CsPbBr3 $@$ FDTS NCs. The FDTS dispersant should be freshly prepared before use ( $10\;\mathrm{mL}$ chloroform, $30~\upmu\mathrm{L}$ ethanol, $1.5~\upmu\mathrm{L}$ deionized water and 10 $\upmu\mathrm{L}$ FDTS). Then $1~\mathrm{mL}$ chloroform and $1~\mathrm{mL}$ FDTS dispersant were added into the redispersed purified CsPbBr3 NCs chloroform solution ( $2\,\mathrm{mL})$ in sequence with a 5 min interval. After stirring for 10 min, $1~\mathrm{mL}$ chloroform and $1~\mathrm{mL}$ FDTS dispersant were added again. The obtained colloid solution was centrifugated (10,000 rpm, 10 min) to get the CsPbBr3@FDTS NCs, which were used for the TEM characterization (Figure 

1c) and photodetector fabrication. After further drying at $50\,^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$ , the CsPbBr3 $@$ FDTS powders were used for other material characterizations and stability tests. 

Preparation of the CsPbBr3 $@$ FDTS flexible photodetector. The CsPbBr3@FDTS NCs were redispersed in chloroform to form a $10\,\mathrm{g/mL}$ uniform dispersion. Polyimide (PI) membranes $2.5\ \mathrm{cm}\times2.5\ \mathrm{cm}$ ) were cleaned sequentially in acetone, ethanol and deionized water. The CsPbBr3@FDTS film was obtained by the drop-casting method, and then the gold electrodes $\mathrm{\Delta}1\ \mathrm{mm}\times1\ \mathrm{mm}$ ) were deposited on the composite film by thermal evaporation, and the channel length between the Au electrodes is $25\;\upmu\mathrm{m}$ . 

**Characterization**

The morphology of nanocrystals was examined by transmission electron microscopy (TEM, FEI Talos F200X) at $200~\mathrm{kV}.$ The composition was determined by energydispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDS) using the accessory manufactured by Oxford instruments. To characterize the structure, powder X-ray diffraction (XRD) was performed on a Rigaku Smartlab 3kW X-ray diffractometer with $\mathrm{{Cu\K_{\alpha}}}$ radiation $\uplambda=$ 1.54056 Å, 40 kV, $30~\mathrm{~mA}$ , $10^{\circ}\:\mathrm{\min}^{-1}$ from 10 to $60^{\circ}$ ). X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) was carried out on the Thermo ESCALAB 250Xi system. The structure of the composites was analyzed by Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR, Thermo Scientific, Nicolet iS60) with the KBr pellet method. The PL and TRPL properties were determined using a fluorescent spectrophotometer (Hitachi F4600 and Edinburgh FLS-1000) at an excitation wavelength of $365~\mathrm{\Omeganm}$ . The absorption spectra were obtained on the Lambda25 UV-Vis-NIR spectrophotometer. The morphology of the perovskite films was examined by scanning electron microscopy (SEM, Zeiss GeminiSEM 300) and atomic force microscopy (AFM, Bruker, USA). The mechanical properties such as elastic modulus and hardness were detected by a Nanoindenter XP (Bruker Hysitron TI950). Electric and optoelectronic measurements of the fabricated devices were conducted in darkness and recorded by the PDA FS380 semiconductor analyzer. 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/ecff7f895eefd301189f0cd539cf86b3c21ca18096307a0f6ac124cbd21b8b57-1724930511.jpg) 

Figure S1. Schematic illustration of the CsPbBr3 $@$ FDTS NCs synthesis process 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/290e42a245f86dc9d7f65a4debeb966335f76bb3c5cce955b654d77b503256ef-1724930515.jpg) 

Figure S2. Particle size distributions: (a) purified CsPbBr3 NCs and (b) CsPbBr3@FDTS. 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/fb18e4e5c05a155cef0de322b485570ec2fee28b767c1d2d6064b3c21468d897-1724930510.jpg) 

Figure S3. HRTEM images of (a) purified CsPbBr3 and (b) CsPbBr3@FDTS NCs 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/0562bda03c72170949547ab490880fead562a2fb238e7ae1c79012e7c7a015cb-1724930516.jpg) 

Figure S4. EDS elemental maps of CsPbBr3@FDTS NCs. 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/b49d74790bb350904c260f2e63aedf9ca49c31a949fcbb09b5f42f5c0b7bee45-1724930518.jpg) 

Figure S5. Br $3d$ XPS spectra of purified CsPbBr3 NCs and CsPbBr3@FDTS. 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/b101ff61ae8529009f7d5e11f324b97806ff4d381a9f9eba805dd9cd9accbdcf-1724930529.jpg) 

Scheme S1. Formation of the in situ network-like coating of Si-O-Si on CsPbBr3. 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/9ad901d0dc1855c3e32b4518dff5bbf844f15a24a85b60ceb6e66778a1de55a6-1724930519.jpg) 

Figure S6. TEM image of CsPbBr3@FDTS synthesized under ambient conditions $(25\,^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$ and relative humidity of $75\%$ ). 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/994d931c28fd6749ebd9fa7da1e0c30cf9d1301c9b29dfaaf2c91d1ce79b88e5-1724930508.jpg) 

Figure S7. TEM image of CsPbBr3@FDTS synthesized without ethanol addition in a sealed environment. 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/2e704c33bac719b6132f276648ee4d60fc9a645813451cb135738808ba26c495-1724930528.jpg) 
Figure S8. TEM images of the CsPbBr3@FDTS NCs with different amount of FDTS addition 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/da918f94c059c95e4f1a830279fbf0b1677f349463b221ab8a07f468ee540c89-1724930513.jpg) 

Figure S9. The contact angle of the CsPbBr3 $@$ FDTS NCs. 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/0920545fbc10a23658c60ee4f2fab1813b59abd76e2241db06c03a8a049e2906-1724930523.jpg) 
Wavelength (nm) 

Figure S10. The PL signal of the purified $\mathrm{Cs}\mathrm{Pb}{\mathrm{Br}}_{3}$ and $\mathrm{CsPbBr}_{3}@]$ FDTS. 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/589e5b59f87b5003362e5e8d321f2f87d7f1399f27f4490e96e362ec4529a4be-1724930530.jpg) 

Figure S11. Time-resolved PL decay and fitted curve of $\mathrm{Cs}\mathrm{Pb}{\mathrm{Br}}_{3}$ OA/OAm. 

Table S1. Fitting parameters and average lifetimes of CsPbBr3 NCs. 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2025/01/10/bc72cff97c5a6f96880691b8f8159505d996d89954315d12c5da3d1ed6be5d20-1736510662.jpg) 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/0d5b153b358bc8af318a683e7b3a4126d1d07639a0ca39bc9a1bf514f9af0b49-1724930527.jpg) 

Figure S12. Time-dependent PL intensity of the perovskite NCs under harsh conditions $85\,^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$ high temperature and $85\%$ relative humidity). 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/72003e3901a9dd38d8224bca3afcbf5c005ce001914539b4feeb46472936e93e-1724930524.jpg) 

Figure S13. SEM images: (a) purified CsPbBr3 and (b) CsPbBr3 $@$ FDTS film. 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/ce25b1e231514c6cfffd3592edb7ef92665a52ba98a313e2578a57b4bcf2f516-1724930525.jpg) 

Figure S14. I–V curves: (a) purified CsPbBr3 and (b) CsPbBr3 $@$ FDTS photodetectors under different illumination intensities at a bias of $5\;\mathrm{V}.$ 

Table S2. The performance of detectors fabricated by $\mathrm{CsPbBr}_{3}@$ FDTS with different parameters. 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/e0b17462d937309c21ebee087acefadf5eddb37e3fd7d5e55b7bdec2f1045675-1724930514.jpg) 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/54e5c54ae30ba6e008b99e934a3bd649fed7ebe5d66e268067b8e5305e3572c7-1724930510.jpg) 

Figure S15. Photocurrent and switch ratio of the purified $\mathrm{Cs}\mathrm{Pb}{\bf B}{\bf r}_{3}$ NCs devices as a function of light intensity at a bias of $5\;\mathrm{V}$ . 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/c41618451ed303ff1f06250b7757b2df76c19be4ed47ac065824c429f588cfa9-1724930521.jpg) 

Figure S16. Responsivity and specific detectivity of the purified CsPbBr3 NCs devices as a function of the light intensity at a bias of $5\;\mathrm{V}$ .

Figure S17. Current−time characteristics of the purified $\mathrm{Cs}\mathrm{Pb}{\mathrm{Br}}_{3}$ NCs devices under different illumination intensities at a bias of $5\;\mathrm{V}.$ . 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/f619dc9e2fa3ac0ed5fe065c3d6e1d476a24ee47194fc2565285711c641c3786-1724930517.jpg) 

Figure S18. I–V curves of the purified $\mathrm{Cs}\mathrm{Pb}{\mathrm{Br}}_{3}$ photodetectors under different bending radii. 

Table S3. Summary of the photoresponse performance figure-of-merits of representative photodetectors 

![](https://imgbed.siat-mic.com/i/2024/08/29/9be2fcdbd736c370f1681ef32686b0f30c8bd69e0fa0423560a454ac3083676a-1724930521.jpg)', 'metadata': None, 'embedding': None, 'score': None}, {'id': None, 'title': 'Room temperature synthesis of stable silica-coated CsPbBr3 quantum dots for amplified spontaneous emission.md', 'content': '

2. EXPERIMENT
Chemicals and reagents: $\mathrm{PbBr}_{2}$ $\left(99.99\%\right)$ and CsBr $(99.9\%)$ were purchased from Xi’an Polymer Light Technology Corp. DMF $(99.9\%$ , Sigma-Aldrich). OA $(90\%)$ ), OAm $(80\%{-}90\%)$ , toluene $(99.5\%)$ , and APTES $(99.5\%)$ were obtained from Adamas. All these reagents were used without further purification. 

Synthesis of $\mathrm{Cs}\mathrm{Pb}{\mathrm{Br}}_{3}$ QDs: $\mathrm{PbBr}_{2}$ $\left(0.4\,\mathrm{\mmol}\right)$ ) and CsBr $(0.4\,\mathrm{\mmol})$ were first added in $10\,\mathrm{\mL}$ of DMF and stirred for $1\,\mathrm{~h~}$ to obtain a clear solution. Then OA $\left(0.6\,\mathrm{\mL}\right)$ ) and $\mathrm{OAm}\left(0.2\,\mathrm{mL}\right)$ were added into the precursor solution followed by a stirring for another $30\,\mathrm{\min}$ . After that, $0.5\,\mathrm{\mL}$ of the precursor solution was quickly added in a beaker containing 

**$10~\mathrm{~mL}$ of toluene under vigorous stirring at $1500~\mathrm{\r/min}$ for $10~s$ .**

Synthesis of $\mathrm{CsPbBr}_{3}@\mathrm{SiO}_{2}$ QDs: $0.5~\mathrm{mL}$ precursor solution was quickly added into $10\;\mathrm{mL}$ toluene containing $0.69\,\upmu\mathrm{L}$ APTES under vigorous stirring at $1500~\mathrm{r/min}$ for $10~s$ . All the above experiments were carried out at room temperature. 

Characterizations: The X-ray diffraction (XRD) characterization was performed using XRD-6100 (Shimadzu, Japan). Fourier transform infrared (FTIR) spectra of the samples were recorded with a Nicolet iS50 (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Waltham, MA, USA). X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) profiles were measured on an ESCA Lab220I-XL. The transmission electron microscopy (TEM) was performed using an electron microscope (Libra 200 FE, Zeiss, Germany). Absorption spectrum was enforced by a UV-2100 (Shimadzu, Japan). The photoluminescence spectrum was obtained by a fluorescence spectrophotometer (Agilent Cary Eclipse, Australia) equipped with a Xe lamp. Time-resolved fluorescence spectra were recorded with a GL-3300 (Photon Technology International Inc., USA). The PLQY was investigated by an FLSP920 (Edinburgh Instruments Ltd., UK). The ASE measurements were performed using a pumping source of a Ti:sapphire amplifier system (wavelength: $800\ \mathrm{nm}$ , repetition rate: $1\ \mathrm{kHz}$ , pulse-width: 50 fs, Solstice, SpectraPhysics). The ASE was detected by a fiber spectrometer (Ocean Optics) with a spectral resolution of $1\,\textrm{n m}$ .', 'metadata': None, 'embedding': None, 'score': None}]

content:
I have found relevant references from the retrieved data regarding the synthesis of CsPbBr₃ nanocubes at room temperature. With careful analysis and a step-by-step approach, I will now design a synthesis strategy for CsPbBr₃ nanocubes specifically focused on being performed at room temperature.

### References and Analysis:
1. **Reference in Title: Ligand-mediated synthesis of shape-controlled CsPbBr₃ nanocrystals**
   - Description mentions PbBr₂ (0.1 mmol), oleic acid (1.55 mmol), dodecylamine (0.086 mmol), N,N-Dimethylformamide (DMF as solvent), and Cs−oleate solution. These materials are instrumental in the preparation of CsPbBr₃ nanocubes.
   - The procedure does not explicitly ensure room temperature.

2. **Reference in Title: Room temperature synthesis of stable silica-coated CsPbBr₃ quantum dots**
   - In this study, CsBr and PbBr₂ are dissolved in DMF followed by adding oleic acid (OA) and oleylamine (OAm) as stabilizing agents. Thereafter, under vigorous stirring and at room temperature, the precursor solution is introduced into toluene. This method explicitly suggests room-temperature synthesis and ensures stability.

3. Additional information provided suggests possible modifications with silane coupling agents (APTES), but I will focus on synthesizing pure CsPbBr₃ nanocubes without additional coatings.

### Final Plan Crafting:
From the extensive reviews of the retrieved data, Reference 2 best matches the requirement for synthesizing CsPbBr₃ nanocubes explicitly at room temperature while remaining simple and scalable.

---

### Comprehensive Synthesis Strategy for CsPbBr₃ Nanocubes (Room Temperature)

1. **Synthesis Conditions**  
   - Solvent: DMF and toluene.  
   - Reaction temperature: **Ambient temperature (~25°C)**.  
   - Stirring: High-speed stirring at 1500 rpm for uniform distribution.  
   - Reaction under atmosphere (no inert gas required).  

2. **Materials & Amounts Required**
| Mat.ID        | Mat.Name          | Mat.Value                  | Mat.Unit       |
| ------------- | ----------------- | -------------------------- | -------------- |
| M001          | PbBr₂             | 0.4                        | mmol           |
| M002          | CsBr              | 0.4                        | mmol           |
| M003          | N,N-Dimethylformamide (DMF) | 10                      | mL            |
| M004          | Oleic Acid (OA)   | 0.6                        | mL            |
| M005          | Oleylamine (OAm)  | 0.2                        | mL            |
| M006          | Toluene           | 10                         | mL            |

3. **Equipment & Containers**
| ID             | Name             | Param/Capacity      | Note                         |
| -------------- | ---------------- | ------------------- | --------------------------- |
| E001           | Magnetic Stirrer | 600-1500 rpm range  | Uniform stirring environment |
| C001           | Glass Beaker     | 20 mL               | For precursor mixing         |
| C002           | Glass Beaker     | 50 mL               | For final reaction           |

4. **Synthesis Sequence**
   1. **Precursor Preparation**: Mixing and dissolving PbBr₂, CsBr in DMF, followed by the addition of OA and OAm.  
   2. **Nanocube Formation**: Introducing this precursor solution into vigorously stirred toluene under ambient temperature and observing immediate reaction and dispersion.

5. **Detailed, Step-by-Step Process**
#### Step-1: Preparation of Precursor
- Measure 0.4 mmol PbBr₂ (M001) and 0.4 mmol CsBr (M002).
- Add these salts to 10 mL of DMF (M003) in a glass beaker (C001).
- Stir the solution at room temp for 1 hour until fully dissolved.
- Add 0.6 mL of oleic acid (M004) and 0.2 mL of oleylamine (M005) to stabilize and further dissolve salts.
- Stir the precursor for an additional 30 minutes.

#### Step-2: Formation of CsPbBr₃ Nanocubes
- Take a 50 mL glass beaker (C002) and add 10 mL toluene (M006).
- Under vigorous stirring (1500 rpm using E001), quickly add 0.5 mL of prepared precursor solution from Step-1 to the beaker (C002).
- Observe the immediate formation of CsPbBr₃ nanocubes as evidenced by a color change (bright color with photoluminescent properties).

#### Step-3: Post-Synthesis Stabilization (optional)
- The colloidal solution can be centrifuged if desired, to separate out nanocubes (~10,000 rpm for 10 minutes). The precipitate can then be re-dispersed in an appropriate solvent for further use.
   
6. **Characterization**
   - Employ standard methods like XRD, TEM, and PL (photoluminescence) spectroscopy to validate the morphology, crystal structure, and optical properties.
   - Desired nanocube structural integrity and emission spectra must match CsPbBr₃ standards.

7. **Other Considerations**
   - Safety: Proper ventilation for handling DMF and toluene.
   - Storage: Keep synthesized nanocubes in toluene under inert conditions to ensure stability and prevent degradation over time.

I will now hand off back to Scientist_Admin for further steps in the synthesis validation process.

content: Transferred to Scientist_Admin, adopting the role of Scientist_Admin immediately.

### Comprehensive Synthesis Strategy for CsPbBr₃ Nanocubes at Room Temperature

1. **Synthesis Conditions**  
   - **Solvent**: N,N-Dimethylformamide (DMF) and Toluene  
   - **Reaction Temperature**: Ambient (25°C)  
   - **Stirring**: High-speed stirring (~1500 rpm) to ensure uniform mixing  
   - **Atmosphere**: Ambient, no specific inert gas required  

2. **Materials**
   - **Lead bromide (PbBr₂)**: 0.4 mmol  
   - **Cesium bromide (CsBr)**: 0.4 mmol  
   - **N,N-Dimethylformamide (DMF)**: 10 mL  
   - **Oleic acid (OA)**: 0.6 mL  
   - **Oleylamine (OAm)**: 0.2 mL  
   - **Toluene**: 10 mL  

3. **Equipment**
   - Magnetic stirrer with 600-1500 rpm range  
   - Glass beakers (20 mL and 50 mL for precursor mixing and reaction, respectively)  

4. **Procedure**

   **Step 1: Precursor Preparation**  
   - Dissolve 0.4 mmol PbBr₂ and 0.4 mmol CsBr in 10 mL of DMF. Stir the solution for 1 hour at room temperature until fully dissolved.  
   - Add 0.6 mL oleic acid and 0.2 mL oleylamine to the solution as stabilizing agents. Stir the precursor solution for an additional 30 minutes.  

   **Step 2: Nanocube Formation**  
   - In a separate 50 mL beaker, add 10 mL of toluene.  
   - Under vigorous stirring (~1500 rpm), quickly inject 0.5 mL of the prepared precursor solution into the toluene.  
   - Observe an immediate color change, indicating the formation of CsPbBr₃ nanocubes.  

   **Step 3: Optional Purification**  
   - Centrifuge the colloidal solution (~10,000 rpm) for 10 minutes to collect the nanocubes.  
   - Re-disperse the precipitate in toluene or another suitable solvent for storage or further use.  

5. **Characterization**
   - Validate the product's morphology, crystal structure, and optical properties using techniques like X-ray diffraction (XRD), transmission electron microscopy (TEM), and photoluminescence spectroscopy (PL).  

6. **Handling and Storage**
   - Ensure proper ventilation when using DMF and toluene.  
   - Store the synthesized nanocubes in toluene under an inert atmosphere to maintain stability and prevent degradation over time.  

This method facilitates the synthesis of CsPbBr₃ nanocubes at room temperature with a focus on simplicity and reproducibility.
